data.requirement: Extract water usage efficiency by computing derived values such as 'Yield per unit of Water Usage' for each crop and district by joining relevant columns from the 'water_usage_data' (Water Consumption, Water Availability) and 'crop_production_data' (Yield, Area) tables.
data.reason: Understanding water usage efficiency is critical for evaluating sustainable water management practices as per the analysis goal.

data.requirement: Highlight geographical variations by aggregating water efficiency, irrigation methods, and yield data by district.
data.reason: Geographical trends can reveal location-specific behaviors and allow recommendations tailored to particular districts.

data.requirement: Filter the data to focus on specific irrigation methods and compute their median water consumption and efficiency metrics aggregated across districts and crop types.
data.reason: Different irrigation methods may have varying impacts on water efficiency. Filtering and aggregating this data will support better decision-making.

data.requirement: Guide the user from macro to micro-level by providing an overview of water usage across crops, districts, and irrigation methods, with the ability to drill down into specific crop-district combinations.
data.reason: This hierarchical approach ensures both breadth and depth of analysis, making it accessible and comprehensive.

data.requirement: Identify outliers or anomalies in water efficiency metrics to flag districts or crops that exhibit unusually high or low use of water relative to yield.
data.reason: Highlighting anomalies can direct attention to inefficiencies or best practices worth emulating.

data.requirement: Correlate soil quality characteristics like pH Level, Organic Matter, and Nutrient Content (from 'soil_analysis_data') with water efficiency and yield metrics.
data.reason: Soil properties may drive water efficiency and yield outcomes; including this correlation supports holistic recommendations for sustainable practices.

data.requirement: Determine seasonal trends in water efficiency by analyzing seasonal data from 'crop_production_data' and its interaction with irrigation methods.
data.reason: Seasonal changes may provide actionable insights into optimizing water usage differently across the year.

data.requirement: Normalize numerical data such as Water Consumption, Water Availability, and Yield for direct comparison and better visualization.
data.reason: Normalization ensures that scale differences do not bias the analysis, making comparisons clearer.

visualization.requirement: Create a box plot to display the distribution of 'Yield per unit of Water Usage' across different irrigation methods.
visualization.reason: This visualization helps characterize the range, median, and variability of water efficiency across irrigation techniques.

visualization.requirement: Use a scatter plot to explore correlations between soil quality (e.g., pH Level, Organic Matter) and 'Water Usage Efficiency'.
visualization.reason: Scatter plots effectively represent relationships between numerical variables, aiding in identifying correlations.

visualization.requirement: Design a bar chart to compare average 'Yield per unit of Water Usage' and 'Water Consumption' across districts.
visualization.reason: Bar charts are intuitive for comparing aggregated metrics across categorical variables like districts.

visualization.requirement: Implement a combined line chart and area chart to analyze seasonal trends in water efficiency and yield for specific crops.
visualization.reason: Combined charts effectively show temporal trends while allowing comparison of two metrics over time.

visualization.requirement: Include a heat map to visualize the efficiency of water usage (e.g., Yield per liter) for different crop-district combinations.
visualization.reason: Heat maps provide a compact and clear way to identify patterns or hotspots in water efficiency across multiple dimensions.

visualization.requirement: Use a pie chart to show the proportion of different irrigation methods used across all districts.
visualization.reason: Pie charts simplify the representation of parts-to-whole relationships, such as the share of irrigation methods.

visualization.requirement: Visualize anomalies in water usage efficiency with a scatter plot overlaid with outlier markers.
visualization.reason: This highlights deviations and informs corrective action or modeling practices.

visualization.requirement: Add a tick chart to indicate water availability vs. consumption balance for each irrigation method.
visualization.reason: Tick charts are ideal for juxtaposing related numerical values in a visually clean manner.

interaction.requirement: Allow users to filter the dataset by district, crop type, and irrigation method dynamically.
interaction.reason: Interactive filters help users focus on specific subsets of the data for targeted analysis and retrieve relevant insights.

interaction.requirement: Enable tooltips on all visualizations to display detailed metrics like exact water usage, yield, and efficiency values on hover.
interaction.reason: Tooltips provide micro-level insights without cluttering the visualization space.

interaction.requirement: Provide brushing and linking functionality between scatter plots and maps, allowing users to select districts or crops and see related data across visualizations.
interaction.reason: This coordination across views allows users to see spatial and relational data simultaneously and enhances understanding.

interaction.requirement: Allow sorting of districts and irrigation methods based on derived efficiency metrics in bar and heat map visualizations.
interaction.reason: Sorting facilitates quick identification of the most and least efficient entities, streamlining decision-making.

interaction.requirement: Support drill-down interactions in heat maps to view detailed time series data for selected crop-district pairs.
interaction.reason: Drill-down functionality aids users in exploring specific instances in greater depth when patterns of interest are identified.

interaction.requirement: Enable the user to toggle between normalized and raw data views for water efficiency and yield metrics.
interaction.reason: This flexibility helps users analyze the data more effectively based on the context of their investigations.

interaction.requirement: Provide a slider for temporal analysis to allow users to adjust the time window and observe seasonal trends interactively.
interaction.reason: Temporal sliders make it easy to explore changes over time, which is particularly useful for season-dependent metrics.

interaction.requirement: Incorporate a comparison mode where users can select two districts or crops to visualize side-by-side differences in water usage efficiency and yield.
interaction.reason: Comparative analysis enhances understanding of relative performance and highlights best practices or areas for improvement.
